vulnerability.reference: Researchers and students treat LLM outputs as authoritative, failing to verify generated references.
vulnerability.generated: Over-reliance on LLM safety alignment by researchers and students, leading to uncritical acceptance of generated content without verification.

impact.reference: Publication of fraudulent references degrades scientific knowledge bases and undermines peer review.
impact.generated: Fabricated references and unethical advice infiltrate academic work, causing publication of flawed research and erosion of scholarly trust.

dose_response.reference: Subversion of LLM safety by adversarial prompts enables generation of fabricated references, which in academic research tools exposes overreliant researchers to false citations, degrading scientific knowledge and causing misconduct.
dose_response.generated: Gradient-optimized adversarial prompts subvert LLM safety alignment when researchers and students use LLMs for literature review, triggering over-reliance and uncritical acceptance, leading to fabricated references and unethical advice infiltrating academic work and eroding scholarly trust.
